title: NASA's Swift Satellite Needs a Rescue Mission
summary: NASA's Swift satellite is an old space telescope that studies powerful explosions in space called gamma-ray bursts. It has been working for over 20 years, but now it is slowly falling out of orbit and could crash to Earth soon. A company named Katalyst Space Technologies is trying to save it by sending a robot spacecraft called Link to meet Swift in space. Link will dock with Swift and push it higher to keep it safe. This rescue mission is very tricky because Swift was not made to be captured, and Katalyst has never done this before. They must launch by June to have enough time before Swift gets too low. If successful, Swift can continue its important science work, helping scientists learn more about the universe.

background_read: The Swift satellite is a NASA space telescope launched in 2004 to watch gamma-ray bursts, which are huge explosions in space. It helps scientists learn about black holes and stars. Over time, satellites like Swift can fall closer to Earth due to drag from the atmosphere. Rescue missions in space are new and risky, often done by companies instead of astronauts to save money. This story shows how technology can help extend the life of important science tools.
Article_Structure: WHO: NASA and Katalyst Space Technologies are involved in saving the Swift satellite.
WHAT: A rescue mission using a robot spacecraft to dock with and boost Swift's orbit.
WHERE: In low-Earth orbit around Earth.
WHY: To prevent Swift from crashing and continue its science on gamma-ray bursts.

perspectives.description: Swift provides valuable data on gamma-ray bursts that helps understand the universe.
The rescue is difficult because Swift wasn't built for docking and Katalyst has never done this before.
